audit_items.proof_trace: Challenge hash uses first num_commitments elements from original commitments_bytes array instead of deduplicated unique_commitments array. When duplicate commitments exist (e.g., [A,B,A,C]), hash incorrectly includes [A,B,A] instead of [A,B,C], allowing malleability attacks where different commitment orderings with same cryptographic content produce different challenges.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker provides cells with commitments [A,B,A,C] vs [A,B,C,A]. Both deduplicate to [A,B,C] for verification, but hash different prefixes ([A,B,A] vs [A,B,C]), producing different Fiat-Shamir challenges despite identical cryptographic content, enabling selective forgery by searching for favorable challenge values.
